- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	# WP-028 — CHR-V3-04 + EDT-V3-03: Cmd+K Command Palette
2	
3	**Branch:** `feat/v3-chr-v3-04-cmd-palette`
4	**Effort:** M — ~2 weeks
5	**State:** `now` (no deps)
6	
7	---
8	
9	## Required reading
10	
11	Read every file below end-to-end before writing a single line of code.
12	
13	- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/spec/workpackage-tracker-v3.md` — §UX CHR-V3-04 entry (line 344) + §6 T2 EDT-V3-03 entry (line 977)
14	- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/CLAUDE.md`
15	- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/packages/web/src/workspace/Workspace.tsx` — top-level chrome; palette mounts here
16	- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/packages/web/src/workspace/StatusBar.tsx` — already has lens dropdown (CHR-V3-03); palette is a sibling concern
17	- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/packages/core/src/index.ts` — existing types; no new kernel types needed for this WP
18	
19	Note: CHR-V3-04 (chrome placement + keybinding) and EDT-V3-03 (commands engine + registry) ship together as one WP — the registry without the chrome is invisible; the chrome without the registry is empty.
20	
21	---
22	
23	## Verbatim tracker entries
24	
25	**CHR-V3-04 — Cmd+K command palette (line 344)**
26	
27	**Status.** `now`.
28	**Scope.** Wire palette to status bar slot 5. OS-aware keybinding (`Cmd+K` Mac / `Ctrl+K` Win/Linux). Universal escape hatch across all themes.
29	**Data model.** Reuses `cmdPalette/registry.ts`.
30	**Engine.** Reuses fuzzysort + recency slice.
31	**UI.** Centered 480 px modal; canvas dims 40 %; results `--text-sm`. Three categories: Commands / Navigate / Select.
32	**Acceptance.** `Cmd+K` opens anywhere; `"phase"` → top match invokes; `Esc` closes. Cross-platform verified.
33	**Effort.** M.
34	
35	**EDT-V3-03 — `Ctrl+K` universal command palette (line 977)**
36	
37	**Status.** `now`.
38	**Scope.** Modal-but-non-blocking palette. Fuzzy-searches commands, views, elements. Three result categories: **Commands**, **Navigate**, **Select**. Ranked by recency × fuzzy × surface-mass. `Esc` closes; `Enter` invokes; arrows cycle.
39	**Data model.** `packages/web/src/cmdPalette/registry.ts` exposes `registerCommand({id, label, keywords, shortcut, invoke, isAvailable})`. Toolbar buttons register; engine commands codegened.
40	**Engine.** Fuzzy via `fuzzysort`. Recency in zustand slice `cmdPaletteRecency` (last 100 invocations, exponential decay over 14 days). `isAvailable(context)` predicates.
41	**UI.** 480 px × max 60 vh centred. Search input `--text-base`. Result list `--text-sm` — label + match highlight + shortcut chip. Section dividers.
42	**Effort.** M — 2 weeks.
43	
44	---
45	
46	## Setup
47	
48	```bash
49	git fetch origin && git checkout main && git pull --ff-only
50	git checkout -b feat/v3-chr-v3-04-cmd-palette
51	cd /Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai
52	uv sync && pnpm install
53	```
54	
55	---
56	
57	## Implementation tasks
58	
59	### 1. Registry module (`packages/web/src/cmdPalette/registry.ts`)
60	
61	```ts
62	export type PaletteEntry = {
63	  id: string;
64	  label: string;
65	  keywords?: string[];
66	  shortcut?: string;
67	  category: 'command' | 'navigate' | 'select';
68	  invoke: (context: PaletteContext) => void;
69	  isAvailable?: (context: PaletteContext) => boolean;
70	};
71	
72	export type PaletteContext = {
73	  selectedElementIds: string[];
74	  activeViewId: string | null;
75	};
76	
77	const _registry: PaletteEntry[] = [];
78	
79	export function registerCommand(entry: PaletteEntry): void {
80	  _registry.push(entry);
81	}
82	
83	export function queryPalette(
84	  input: string,
85	  context: PaletteContext,
86	  recency: Record<string, number>,
87	): PaletteEntry[] {
88	  // Filter by isAvailable; fuzzy-sort by label+keywords; boost by recency
89	  ...
90	}
91	```
92	
93	Use `fuzzysort` (already a dependency — verify in `package.json`, install if missing).
94	
95	### 2. Recency store (`packages/web/src/cmdPalette/paletteRecencyStore.ts`)
96	
97	Zustand slice:
98	- `invocations: Record<string, { ts: number; count: number }>` (last 100).
99	- `recordInvocation(id: string)` — upsert; trim to 100; persist to `localStorage`.
100	- `getRecencyScore(id: string, now: number): number` — exponential decay over 14 days: `count * exp(-decay * (now - ts) / (14 * 86400 * 1000))`.
101	- Hydrates from `localStorage` on init.
102	
103	### 3. Palette component (`packages/web/src/cmdPalette/CommandPalette.tsx`)
104	
105	```tsx
106	// Centred modal, 480 px wide, max 60 vh tall.
107	// Canvas backdrop dims to 40% opacity (CSS backdrop filter or overlay div).
108	// Three sections: Commands / Navigate / Select.
109	// Keyboard: ArrowDown/Up cycle; Enter invokes top; Esc closes.
110	// Each result row: label + match highlight + shortcut chip at --text-sm.
111	// Section dividers at --text-2xs.
112	```
113	
114	Props:
115	```ts
116	type CommandPaletteProps = {
117	  isOpen: boolean;
118	  onClose: () => void;
119	  context: PaletteContext;
120	};
121	```
122	
123	State: controlled by parent (Workspace.tsx or a global `usePaletteStore` zustand slice).
124	
125	### 4. Keybinding + mount (`packages/web/src/workspace/Workspace.tsx`)
126	
127	Add a `useEffect` in `Workspace.tsx`:
128	
129	```ts
130	useEffect(() => {
131	  const handler = (e: KeyboardEvent) => {
132	    if ((e.metaKey || e.ctrlKey) && e.key === 'k') {
133	      e.preventDefault();
134	      openPalette();
135	    }
136	  };
137	  window.addEventListener('keydown', handler);
138	  return () => window.removeEventListener('keydown', handler);
139	}, [openPalette]);
140	```
141	
142	Mount `<CommandPalette>` at the root of `Workspace.tsx` (outside the canvas layers so it overlays everything).
143	
144	### 5. Seed registry entries
145	
146	Register at minimum these entries in a `packages/web/src/cmdPalette/defaultCommands.ts` file that is imported once at app boot:
147	
148	```ts
149	// Commands
150	registerCommand({ id: 'tool.wall', label: 'Place Wall', shortcut: 'W', keywords: ['wall', 'draw'], category: 'command', invoke: ctx => ... });
151	registerCommand({ id: 'tool.door', label: 'Place Door', shortcut: 'D', category: 'command', invoke: ctx => ... });
152	registerCommand({ id: 'view.phase.demolition', label: 'Set view phase: Demolition', keywords: ['phase', 'demolition'], category: 'command', invoke: ctx => ... });
153	registerCommand({ id: 'view.phase.existing', label: 'Set view phase: Existing', keywords: ['phase', 'existing'], category: 'command', invoke: ctx => ... });
154	registerCommand({ id: 'view.phase.new', label: 'Set view phase: New Construction', keywords: ['phase', 'new'], category: 'command', invoke: ctx => ... });
155	// Navigate (example structure — populate with real view kinds)
156	registerCommand({ id: 'navigate.plan', label: 'Go to plan view', category: 'navigate', invoke: ctx => ... });
157	```
158	
159	Add more as appropriate — the registry is extensible; this seeds the `Acceptance` test cases.
160	
161	### 6. Tests (`packages/web/src/cmdPalette/registry.test.ts`)
162	
163	Vitest unit tests (or Jest — match existing test runner in `package.json`):
164	
165	- `queryPalette('phase', ...)` returns `Set view phase: Demolition` in top 3.
166	- `queryPalette('ww', ...)` with 2 walls selected returns `Lock parallel` in top 3 (if registered).
167	- `queryPalette('', ...)` returns all available entries sorted by recency score.
168	- `queryPalette('garbage_xyz_abc', ...)` returns empty array.
169	- `isAvailable` predicate gates entries correctly.
170	- Recency score is higher for recently invoked entries.
171	
172	---
173	
174	## Implementation rules
175	
176	- **OS-aware keybinding.** Use `e.metaKey` for Mac, `e.ctrlKey` for Win/Linux. `navigator.platform` or `navigator.userAgent` to distinguish. Both must work.
177	- **No hex literals.** All colours in TSX/CSS use CSS token variables.
178	- **`--radius-canvas: 0` exception.** The palette is a floating modal, not a canvas — use `--radius-card` (if it exists) or `border-radius: 4px`. Do NOT use `--radius-canvas: 0` here.
179	- **fuzzysort.** If not in `package.json`, add it: `pnpm add fuzzysort`. Do not vendor it.
180	- **Registry is append-only at runtime.** `registerCommand` pushes; nothing removes at runtime. Clearing is only in tests.
181	
182	---
183	
184	## Verify gate
185	
186	```bash
187	cd /Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai
188	pnpm test --filter web -- cmdPalette       # unit tests
189	pnpm tsc --noEmit
190	pnpm format:check
191	```
192	
193	If the project doesn't have a unit test runner set up yet, write the tests as Vitest files and ensure `vitest` is in `devDependencies`.
194	
195	---
196	
197	## Commit and push
198	
199	```bash
200	git add packages/web/src/cmdPalette/ \
201	        packages/web/src/workspace/Workspace.tsx
202	git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
203	feat(chr): CHR-V3-04+EDT-V3-03 Cmd+K command palette — registry + recency + fuzzy
204	
205	cmdPalette/registry.ts with registerCommand + queryPalette (fuzzysort + recency decay);
206	paletteRecencyStore zustand slice (exponential 14-day decay, localStorage persistence);
207	CommandPalette.tsx modal (480 px, 40% backdrop dim, 3 categories); Cmd+K keybinding
208	in Workspace.tsx; seed defaultCommands.ts with wall/door/phase entries.
209	
210	Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
211	EOF
212	)"
213	git push -u origin feat/v3-chr-v3-04-cmd-palette
214	```
215	
216	---
217	
218	## Final report template
219	
220	```
221	WP: WP-028 / CHR-V3-04 + EDT-V3-03
222	Branch: feat/v3-chr-v3-04-cmd-palette
223	Status: done
224	Tests: <N> passed (registry.test.ts)
225	Files changed:
226	  - packages/web/src/cmdPalette/registry.ts
227	  - packages/web/src/cmdPalette/paletteRecencyStore.ts
228	  - packages/web/src/cmdPalette/CommandPalette.tsx
229	  - packages/web/src/cmdPalette/defaultCommands.ts
230	  - packages/web/src/cmdPalette/registry.test.ts
231	  - packages/web/src/workspace/Workspace.tsx  (Cmd+K keybinding + mount)
232	Notes: <anything unexpected — fuzzysort version issues, keybinding conflicts, etc.>
233	
234	Tracker: §UX CHR-V3-04 → done; §6 T2 EDT-V3-03 → done
235	```

285:`Cmd+K` on Mac, `Ctrl+K` on Windows / Linux (R-A §3; EDT-V3-03). Modal-but-non-blocking: canvas dims 40 %, 480 px × max-60vh panel centres. Three categories: **Commands** (`"phase"` → "Set view phase: Demolition"), **Navigate** (`"A101"` → "Open sheet A101"), **Select** (`"kit"` → "Element: kitchen 02"). Ranked recency × fuzzy × surface-mass.
344:#### CHR-V3-04 — Cmd+K command palette
346:**Status.** `now`. Cross-ref T2 EDT-V3-03 (commands engine; this WP is chrome placement + cross-platform binding).
924:| EDT-V3-03  | `Ctrl+K` universal command palette                                    | M      | now      | —                            |
977:#### EDT-V3-03 — `Ctrl+K` universal command palette
1218:- **A3 Ribbon-with-30-tabs** — _Antidote:_ `Ctrl+K` command palette (EDT-V3-03) plus a context-sensitive panel; toolbars carry only the ~10 most-used tools per surface, every other command is a palette query.
1227:The cursor-language + command-grammar + associative-dimensions trio is what makes the editing feel _alive_ at Rayon-grade. Cursor language (EDT-V3-02) turns the cursor into a feedback channel — the user always knows what was acquired and whether the gesture is associative. Command grammar (EDT-V3-03 + 04 + 05 + 12) turns the keyboard into a verb — single-letter shortcuts visible in chrome, `Ctrl+K` as universal escape hatch, mid-command modifiers, numeric override during draw — so the architect's hands never leave home row. Associative dimensions (EDT-V3-07) make geometry and dimension labels move as one unit. The three compound: without cursor language the user can't tell when a snap is associative; without command grammar they can't reach the right tool fast enough; without associative dimensions every geometry edit invalidates downstream documentation. Underneath sits **EDT-V3-01 — the four constraint rules** v2 left as pass-through. Without parallel / perpendicular / collinear / equal-length the pipeline still feels like CAD; with them it feels like _drafting in conversation with the model_. That's why EDT-V3-01 is T2's keystone and R-E's highest-priority single carry-forward.
3654:| CHR-V3-04 Cmd+K palette            | T2 EDT-V3-03 (cmd palette spec) + API-V3-01 | Hard    | Palette commands invoke API-V3-01 endpoints by name.                                                                                  |
3683:**T2 (in-place UX):** EDT-V3-01 (constraint rules — _highest carry-forward priority from R-E_), EDT-V3-03 (Cmd/Ctrl+K command palette spec), EDT-V3-04 (single-letter shortcuts in tooltips), EDT-V3-05 (loop-mode mid-modifier), EDT-V3-06 (helper dimensions on selection).
3709:**After EDT-V3-03 + API-V3-01:** CHR-V3-04 (Cmd+K palette), CHR-V3-08 (secondary contextual bar) — 2 WPs.
3797:| Deep new family-editor parametric capabilities (NEW parameter types beyond v2's `string`/`number`/`yes-no`/`length`/`angle`/`material`/`type`/`array`/`conditional`/`URL` shipped in FAM-01..10) | v2 shipped the family-editor depth bet. v3 confines family work to **UX consistency with the project canvas** (cursor language, command grammar, padlock glyph in the family editor match the project canvas — handled inside T2 EDT-V3-01 + EDT-V3-03). _New_ parameter types (fluid parameters, stochastic distributions, native ML-driven flex) are post-v3 — they belong in the same future window as embedded AI. |
